title: Graph Theory, Networks, Scheduling, and Routing · Quantity Clinic: Network Tradeoffs
summary: Design routes and schedules for deliveries and shared services. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int network tradeoffs increment in a network routing and scheduling analysis. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare efficiency, resilience, fairness, and capacity in network decisions.
Compute shortest paths and verify that edge assumptions fit the algorithm.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper
